persona_name: P5 中英夹杂海归 (medium — code-switching)
expected_behavior: Capture ≥6/7 required slots from mixed zh/en answers; preserve technical entities (Jira, k8s, Okta, Redis, Postgres, PROJ-1234, sprint velocity, SSO) verbatim in final spec; terminate cleanly with spec_capsule_cid.
suspected_failure: Triage cascade misclassifies code-switched answers (likely as off_topic or gibberish), driving predicate_double_fail before any slot beyond `job` is accepted. Backend then force-terminates with user_input_unparseable_no_spec, never running synthesis.
actual_behavior: Bootstrap OK and T1 actually advanced normally — the interviewer mirrored back ALL key entities verbatim from the mixed-language T1 (dashboard, sprint velocity, Jira, k8s, Okta), proving the meta LLM itself understands code-switch perfectly. T2 (anchor: 'Anchor 就用 Jira issue key, PROJ-1234, 30-40 tickets / sprint') and T3 (memory: Redis cache / Postgres snapshot) both came back with covered_slots:[] / open_slots:[] / confidence:0.0 / question_text reused-or-empty — the classic F1/F6 silent-zero envelope signature. Server then terminated at T3 with termination_reason='user_input_unparseable_no_spec' and spec_capsule_cid=null. CAS index shows TWO consecutive turn-3-triage EvidenceCapsules — i.e. triage retried once and presumably double-failed — and zero turn-3 main / synth / spec capsules. No spec.md or spec.json was ever written. Entity loss is total beyond turn 1 because the pipeline never reached the synthesis stage.

metrics.envelope_parse_success_rate: 4/4 HTTP 200, but T2 + T3 returned empty envelopes (silent-zero / F6 pattern); only bootstrap + T1 produced semantically meaningful envelopes
metrics.triage_class_distribution: not surfaced in HTTP response; CAS shows 1× turn-2-triage capsule and 2× turn-3-triage capsules (triage retry on T3 → suggests triage_double_fail)
metrics.entity_loss_note: Total loss — no spec was ever synthesised. T1 mirror-back proved the LLM CAN extract code-switched entities, so the loss is not a language-understanding failure of the meta LLM; it is a pipeline-level guard (triage / envelope-parse) tripping on T2+T3.
metrics.language_register_of_mirror_back_t1: zh-dominant with English technical terms preserved verbatim (Jira, k8s, Okta) — desirable behavior for this persona
metrics.lang_predicate_violations: none observed (no P6 LanguageMismatch surfaced; failure happened earlier in the triage/envelope stack)
metrics.narrative: P5 code-switch persona ran 3 user turns and was force-terminated. Turn 1 was a textbook success: full mirror-back preserved all named entities across the language switch and 'job' slot was credited. Turns 2 and 3 — semantically perfect anchor and memory answers in the same code-switch register — produced silent-zero envelopes (covered_slots:[], open_slots:[], confidence:0.0, question_text='' on T3). CAS shows triage was invoked twice on T3 (two turn-3-triage capsules) but no turn-3 main capsule was ever created, and no spec capsule exists. Termination reason 'user_input_unparseable_no_spec' confirms the predicate double-fail termination path. The failure mode is the F1/F6 silent-zero / triage-cascade combination, specifically biased against mid-stream code-switched input (T1 with code-switch passed; T2/T3 with code-switch did not). This is a real Phase 6.3.x universality regression for the code-switch register and should join the failure inventory.

anomalies: T2 returned a full question_text identical to T1 (mirror-back repeated) but covered_slots and open_slots both empty — envelope-parse silent-zero, not a re-ask.
T3 returned question_text='' AND terminated=true in the same envelope — abrupt termination with no farewell, no playback, no spec.
termination_reason='user_input_unparseable_no_spec' is set but spec_capsule_cid is null — server correctly refuses to claim a spec it did not synthesise.
CAS contains two turn-3-triage EvidenceCapsules with identical sizes (993 bytes) and different oids — triage retry / double-fire visible at the tape layer.
No turn-3 main LLM call was ever logged in CAS (no cmd_llm_complete/turn-3 entry) — pipeline halted at the triage stage.
F1/F6 fixes claimed in-flight clearly do NOT cover the code-switch register; triage misclassifies mid-stream code-switch even when answers are technically rich and on-topic.
